nodes.title: Molecular & Cell Biology
nodes.description: Explores the fundamental processes of life. Topics include cellular metabolism; structure and function of organelles; cell cycle; signal transduction; biology of cancer. CHEM 2580, BCOR 2300 recommended. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 2505 and BCOR 2500. Prerequisites: BCOR 1400, BIOL 1400, or BIOL 1000; BCOR 1450, BIOL 1450, BIOL 1005, or BCOR 1425; CHEM 1400, CHEM 1405, or CHEM 1410.
nodes.other_requirements: Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre Med students enroll in BCOR 2505 ZRA

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory-level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: General Chemistry 2
nodes.description: Second semester of a two-semester sequence. Topics include solutions, kinetics, equilibrium, acid-base chemistry, aqueous ionic equilibria, thermodynamics, electrochemistry, and nuclear chemistry. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1450 and CHEM 1455. Prerequisite: CHEM 1400 or CHEM 1405.
nodes.other_requirements: $60 fee for lab supplies; Degree students only;

nodes.title: Exploring Chemistry 2
nodes.description: Second semester of a first-year laboratory addressing foundational chemical principles and experimental methods. For first-year Biochemistry majors also enrolled in CHEM 1550. Prerequisites: CHEM 1500 and CHEM 1410. Co-requisite: CHEM 1550.
nodes.other_requirements: CHEM 1550; BICM majors only; $85 Fee: Chem Lab Supplies PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Ecology and Evolution
nodes.description: Ecosystem and community structure, population growth, species interactions and niche dynamics, population and chromosomal genetics, speciation in fossil records, ecology of animal behavior, applied ecology. Prerequisites: BCOR 1400, BIOL 1400, or BIOL 1000; BCOR 1450, BIOL 1450, BIOL 1005, or BCOR 1425; MATH 1212 or MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: General Chemistry 1
nodes.description: First semester of a two-semester sequence. Topics include matter, stoichiometry, gas laws, thermochemistry, quantum theory, atomic structure, electronic configurations, bonding, and intermolecular forces. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1400 and CHEM 1100, CHEM 1102, or CHEM 1405.

nodes.title: Exploring Chemistry 1
nodes.description: First-year laboratory addressing foundational chemical principles and experimental methods. For first-year Biochemistry majors also enrolled in CHEM 1500. Co-requisite: CHEM 1500.
nodes.other_requirements: For first-year Biochemistry majors also enrolled in CHEM 1500. Co-requisite: CHEM 1500. Coreq:CHEM 1500A; First-time, first-year BICM majors only; $85 fee for chemical lab supplies. PACE students by permission and override. The is the laboratory-component of General Chemistry for Biochemistry majors who have placed out of the lecture-component of General Chemistry based upon their High School preparation. The lab meets once per week. Students are expected to come prepared with proper PPE and write laboratory reports.

nodes.title: Exploring Neuroscience
nodes.description: In-depth survey of neuroscience topics, including neuron function, the anatomical and functional organization of the nervous system, and diseases of the nervous system. No laboratory. Credit not awarded for both NSCI 2100 and NSCI 2105. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400; BIOL 1400, BIOL 1450; or BCOR 1400, BCOR 1450; or BIOL 1000, BIOL 1005; or BIOL 1000, BCOR 1425; or ANPS 1190, ANPS 1200. Co-requisite: CHEM 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: Neuroscience majors and minors. PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Calculus I
nodes.description: Introduction to limits and differential/integral calculus with a wide variety of applications. Students interested in intensive use of mathematics should take MATH 1234. Credit not awarded for both MATH 1212 and MATH 1234 unless followed by MATH 1248. See MATH 1242. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1012 or C- or better in MATH 1034, or a score of 61 or higher on the placement assessment.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1012 or C- or better in MATH 1034, or a score of 61 or higher on the placement assessment. Prereqs enforced by the system: MATH 1012 or MATH 1034 or ALEKS MPA of at least 61; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Genetics
nodes.description: The basis of inheritance, covering topics from classical genetics to modern molecular studies. Analysis of genetic data emphasized, from prokaryotic, animal, and plant systems. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 2300 and ASCI 2160. Prerequisites: BCOR 1400, BIOL 1400, or BIOL 1000; BCOR 1450, BIOL 1450, BIOL 1005, or BCOR 1425; CHEM 1100, CHEM 1400, CHEM 1405, or CHEM 1410.
nodes.other_requirements: Degree students only even after level restrictions removed. Please email Laura.A.Newman@uvm.edu for all registration issues, including using BHSC 1340 in lieu of BIOL/BCOR. Must register for a discussion section A01-A09.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; Contact instructor for meeting location and time.

nodes.title: Molecular & Cell Biology w/lab
nodes.description: Explores the fundamental processes of life. Topics include cellular metabolism; structure and function of organelles; cell cycle; signal transduction; biology of cancer. CHEM 2580, BCOR 2300 recommended. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 2500 and BCOR 2505. Prerequisites: BCOR 1400, BIOL 1400, or BIOL 1000; BCOR 1450, BIOL 1450, BIOL 1005, or BCOR 1425; CHEM 1400 or CHEM 1410.
nodes.other_requirements: For majors: ASCI, BICM, BIOL, BISC, ENSC, MGEN, MICR, NEUR, PBIO, ZOOL only. Minimum sophomore standing. If enrolling in CHEM 1450/1460, please request an override from Laura.A.Newman@uvm.edu. Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre Med students enroll in BCOR 2500 ZRA.

nodes.title: Principles of Biology 2
nodes.description: Principles of organismal biology; nature of scientific inquiry, plant form and function, pollination ecology, animal phylogeny illustrated by comparative anatomy and physiology; animal behavior. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1450 and BCOR 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: ASCI, BIOL, BISC, MGEN, MICR, NEUR, PBIO, ZOOL majors should register for BCOR 1450; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre med students register for BIOL 1450

nodes.title: Accelerated Biology
nodes.description: Selected topics from the full year of introductory biology, compressed into one semester. For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. Pre/co-requisite: CHEM 1400, CHEM 1405, or CHEM 1410.
nodes.other_requirements: For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. By invitation only; for FTFY students with AP score of 4. Instructor permission required. Must register for a BCOR 1425 lab. Must be enrolled in CHEM 1400 if have no CHEM credit.

nodes.title: Principles of Biology 1
nodes.description: Principles of cellular biochemistry; cell biology; genetics and evolution. Topics: biochemistry; metabolism, cell structure/function; respiration; photosynthesis; molecular, Mendelian and population genetics; genetics of evolution. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1400 and BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1425.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1400 and BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1425. Must register for a BIOL 1400 lab; Lab supply fee: $30; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre Med & PACE students register for BIOL 1400 ZRA Colocated with BIOL 1400 ZRA

nodes.title: Outline: General Chem w/lab
nodes.description: One-semester survey of principles and concepts of general chemistry, topics covered include bonding, mole ratios, equilibrium, and nuclear chemistry. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1100 and CHEM 1102, CHEM 1400, or CHEM 1405.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1100 and CHEM 1102, CHEM 1400, or CHEM 1405. $60 fee for lab supplies; Colocated with CHEM 1102A; Must register for CHEM 1100 lab L01-L07; Non-CAS, non - BSAD students only; Open to degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 1
nodes.description: Exploring biology from cells to organisms. Topics include origins of life, ancestral organisms, uni- and multi- cellular energetics, evolution of respiration and metabolism, and the genetic code. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1400 and BIOL 1400 or BCOR 1425.

nodes.title: Calculus I
nodes.description: Introduction to calculus of functions of one variable including: limits, continuity, techniques and applications of differentiation and integration. Credit not given for more than one course in the pair MATH 1212, MATH 1234 unless followed by MATH 1248 or MATH 1242. No credit following receipt of credit for MATH 1248 or above. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1034 or a score of 76 or higher on the placement assessment.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1034 or a score of 76 or higher on the placement assessment. Prereqs enforced by the system.

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 2
nodes.description: An evolutionary perspective to exploring biology. Topics include: patterns of inheritance, Darwinian evolution, evolution of biodiversity, ecology of organisms, human effects on biological systems. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1450 and BIOL 1450.

nodes.title: Biology in Practice
nodes.description: Introduction to a broad array of biological disciplines through attending seminars in the life sciences. The course will introduce students to hypothesis testing and data analysis and interpretation of results, as well as scientific presentation through attending weekly seminars, reading related scientific literature, and participating in class discussions. Prerequisites: (BCOR 2300, BCOR 2100) or (BCOR 2300, BCOR 2500) or (BCOR 2100, BCOR 2500) or (BCOR 2300, NSCI 2105).
nodes.other_requirements: Junior or Senior standing required. Open to PACE students with permission and override
